01 / Why train here

The real thing,
not cardio kickboxing.

Authentic

Thai-tested technique

Coach Anthony has fought at one of the biggest stadiums in Thailand and on MAX Muay Thai. What gets taught on these mats gets tested in real rings.

All levels

Walk in as you are

"Great for all levels," as one student put it. First-timers train next to fighters, and reviews keep repeating the same word: family.

Proving ground

A fight team that wins

Students who want to compete have a real path: the Garden Grove Fight Team, with title fights and an undefeated 6-0 female fighter among them.

02 / The coach

Coach Anthony

"Coach Anthony is very welcoming, and the fighters and students get along really well like family."

That is a real student review, and it sits next to a resume most American gyms cannot claim: Coach Anthony fights in Thailand, has taken a championship belt, and is still active in the ring today.

In the gym he goes by Kru, the Thai word for teacher. Students also call out coach Rachel by name in reviews. Between them: a school where beginners are welcomed and fighters are built.

03 / Programs

Pick your fight.

Adult Muay Thai

Beginner to advanced / evenings

Technique, pads, clinch, and conditioning. Structured classes with theory and practice in every session, whatever your level.

Kids Muay Thai

Ages 4 and up / 5:20 pm

Discipline, coordination, and confidence in a class kids genuinely look forward to. A coach reviews call "interested in helping kids find direction."

Fight Team

By invitation / competitors

For students who want to compete. Real bouts, real corners, real records. This is where the gym's title fights come from.

Class hours
Monday to Thursday, 5:20 pm to 9:00 pm. Kids first, adults after. Text us and we will point you to the right class.

05 / Before you walk in

First-timer questions.

I've never trained before. Will I be out of place?

No. Reviews from complete beginners say they were hooked after one class. Classes run all levels together with work scaled to where you are.

Do I need my own gloves or gear?

Come in athletic clothes with water for your first class. Gear can come later once you know you are in.

How young can kids start?

Kids classes start at age 4, early evening at 5:20 pm so it works after school.

Is it safe? Will I have to spar?

Sparring is never forced on beginners. Classes are technique, pads, and conditioning first. The fight team is a separate, invitation path for people who want to compete.

What does it cost?

Come try a class first, meet the coaches, then talk numbers that fit your schedule. Students in reviews call the pricing good for what you get.

What if I just want to get in shape?

That is most of the gym. You will get cardio, endurance, strength, and speed work in every class. Competing is optional; sweating is not.
